title: Deduplicate swarm stop side effects
goal: Fix a small issue #13 robustness gap in swarm stop idempotency by ensuring duplicate stop retries cannot emit duplicate stop side effects before replaying the persisted response.
requiredInputs: Observed handle_comm_stop side effect before begin_or_replay
permissions: Modify swarm stop handler and tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: `handle_comm_stop` begins or replays its swarm mutation before any stop side effects, including target `SessionCloseRequested` fanout and session/member removal.
Repeating the same coordinator/target stop request after completion replays `Done` without sending another close request to the target event channel.
Authorization/target-resolution errors remain unchanged and do not create mutation state before the target is known and stop is allowed.
Focused stop idempotency test, cargo fmt/check, git diff check, and selfdev build pass.
failureTaxonomy: Moving mutation before authorization and persisting unauthorized stop attempts
Changing stop permissions or force behavior
Returning stale replay for a different target/swarm
Breaking normal first stop removal/member cleanup behavior
Leaving mutation active on early return after begin
notes: Keep the slice limited to stop side-effect ordering and tests. Do not redesign cleanup or stop permissions.
